content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 13, 1986


Michael E. Crantrall
California Public Defenders Association
717 K Street, Room 500
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-86-138

Dear Mr. Crantrall:

This is in reply to your letter of April 23, 1986, regarding the
lobbying provisions of the Political Reform Act.

You have asked if it is permissible for your organization to pay
for legislative advocacy services via another organization that also
contracts with the same legislative advocate, and if possible, the
proper method of reporting.

Your organization, as a lobbyist employer, may make payments to
another organization for lobbying services. When reporting the
payments on your employer report, Form 635, you must report your
payments as if they were made directly to the lobbyist in Part III,
B, Payments to Lobbying Firms, Including Individual Contract
Lobbyists, with a notation that the payments to the lobbyist are
being made through the other organization, i.e., California Attorneys
for Criminal Justice.

You also asked how to report payments to a lobbyist who has
provided office space to your lobbyist. You report this as an
advance or other payment to your lobbyist in Part III B, Column (C)
of your employer report.

I hope the above answers your questions. If you need further
assistance or clarification, please do not hesitate to contact the
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division.

Sincerely,

Mary Ann Kvasager
Political Reform Consultant

MAK:cah


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

April 28, 1986


Michael E. Cantrall
CPDA
717 K Street, Room 500
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: 86-138

Dear Mr. Cantrall:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act has been received by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice
request, you may contact the Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or unless more information is needed to answer your request,
you should expect a response within 21 working days.

Very truly yours,

Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division

APR 29 7 47 AM 86

April 23, 1986

California Fair Political Practices
Commission
Attn: Technical Assistance Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear FPPC:

Our organization is planning to arrange to indirectly pay
for our legislative advocacy services via another
organization that also contracts with the same legislative
advocate. We are wondering if this is permissible. What we
would do is this: We receive representation by a lobbyist,
he bills another organization for the services he renders
for them, and also bills our organization for the services
he renders for our organization. Our organization
then reimburses the other organization for the portion of
the payment owed by us.

Another question that we have is this: How should we report
on the quarterly forms, amounts paid to a lobbyist for
expenses he is billing us for another lobbyist's expenses
(our lobbyist is using another lobbyist's office space and
we are reimbursing him for this third-party, who happens to be a
lobbyist, for the use of the space).

Please feel free to call if you have questions, or need to
ask for clarification on these arrangements.

Sincerely,

[SIGNATURE]

Michael E. Cantrall
Executive Director

sections.question: You have asked if it is permissible for your organization to pay for legislative advocacy services via another organization that also contracts with the same legislative advocate, and if permissible, the proper method of reporting. You also asked how to report payments to a lobbyist who has provided office space to your lobbyist.
sections.conclusion: Your organization, as a lobbyist employer, may make payments to another organization for lobbying services. When reporting the payments on your employer report, Form 635, you must report your payments as if they were made directly to the lobbyist in Part III, B, Payments to Lobbying Firms, Including Individual Contract Lobbyists, with a notation that the payments to the lobbyist are being made through the other organization, i.e., California Attorneys for Criminal Justice. You report this as an advance or other payment to your lobbyist in Part III B, Column (C) of your employer report.
sections.question_synthetic: May a lobbyist employer pay for legislative advocacy services through another organization that contracts with the same lobbyist, and how should such payments be reported on Form 635? Additionally, how should payments for office space provided by a lobbyist be reported?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Payments for advocacy services through another organization are permissible and should be reported on Form 635 Part III, B as if paid directly to the lobbyist, with notation of the intermediary organization. Office space payments should be reported as advances or other payments to the lobbyist in Part III B, Column (C).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes initial acknowledgment letter (April 28, 1986) and substantive advice letter (May 13, 1986) plus original request letter (April 23, 1986). Extracted data from the substantive FPPC response. All key sections identified clearly.

You also asked how to report payments to a lobbyist who has provided office space to your lobbyist.

CONCLUSION: Your organization, as a lobbyist employer, may make payments to another organization for lobbying services. When reporting the payments on your employer report, Form 635, you must report your payments as if they were made directly to the lobbyist in Part III, B, Payments to Lobbying Firms, Including Individual Contract Lobbyists, with a notation that the payments to the lobbyist are being made through the other organization, i.e., California Attorneys for Criminal Justice. You report this as an advance or other payment to your lobbyist in Part III B, Column (C) of your employer report.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the California Public Defenders Association that it may pay for legislative advocacy services through another organization contracting with the same lobbyist, and provides specific reporting instructions for Form 635. The letter also addresses reporting requirements for lobbyist office space payments.
